Use of 푅푅= 푅푅 +푅푅 Use of 푅푅= 푉푉 퐼퐼 to calculate (circuit) current Use of 푅푅= 푉푉 퐼퐼 to calculate p.d. across thermistor p.d. = 0.94 (V) 0.94 (V) is more than 0.90 (V) so heater will not have switched off before water temperature is 65°C Or Comparison of their calculated value of p.d. with 0.90 (V) and consistent conclusion (1) (1) (1) (1) (1) MP2 and MP3 can be awarded for use of potential divider equation as in alternative calculation. Example of calculation 푅푅=56 Ω+300 Ω =356 Ω 퐼퐼= 푉푉 푅푅 = 6 V 356 Ω = 1.69 × 10 −2 A 푉푉 푡푡ℎ 푒푒푟푟푚푚푒푒 푒푒푡푡푒푒푟푟 = 1.69 × 10 −2 A × 56 Ω = 0.94 V 0. 94 V is greater than 0.90 V so heater will not switch off when water temperature is 65°C Alternative calculation 푉푉 = 56 Ω 300 Ω+56 Ω V = 0.94 V 0.94 V is greater than 0.90 V so heater will not switch off when water temperature is 65°C Total for question 17 11 Number Acceptable Answer Additional Guidance Mark Plane polarised has oscillations / vibrations in one plane Which includes the direction of (wave) travel (MP3 dependent on MP1 and MP2 being awarded) Or Unpolarised has oscillations / vibrations in all / many directions Plane polarised has oscillations / vibrations in one direction Which is perpendicular to the direction of (wave) travel (MP3 dependent on MP1 and MP2 being awarded) (1) (1) (1) (1) (1) (1) (as the filter is rotated) a different single plane / direction of oscillation passes through, so the intensity stays the same (1) (1) Filter 1 is aligned with filter 2 so light is transmitted through filter 2 Or filter 2 transmits the light transmitted by filter 1 Filter 3 is perpendicular to filter 2 so no light passes through filter 3 After rotation: some light transmitted through filter 2 is also transmitted through filter 3 So intensity of light transmitted through filter 3 increase (1) (1) (1) (1) (1) Total for question 18 10
